Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

For id=9 at [34-35, 15-16]:
- To reach [23-24, 29-30]: needs to move up 11 rows and right 13 columns. Can't move right!

When I click on a corner of a magenta square, it moves in a direction based on which corner I clicked:

Given this constraint, the target positions must be above and to the left of the current positions. Let me think about what positions make sense.
Actually, wait. I just realized that I can combine moves to achieve different net directions. For example:

- To move left: click bottom-left corner
- To move up-left: click top-left corner
So I can move the squares in any direction that is a combination of up and left moves. This means I can reach any position that is above and to the left of the current position.
Given this, let me think about the target positions again. The magenta squares need to be moved to positions that are above and to the left of their current positions.

- id=9: [34-35, 15-16]
- id=10: [35-36, 39-40]
- id=11: [52-53, 47-48]
- id=12: [53-54, 12-13]
The blue blob is at [23-31, 29-37]. The top-left corner of the blue blob is at [23, 29].
If the magenta squares need to be moved to surround the blue blob, they would need to be at positions like:

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model (revised):
- Movement confirmed: top-left→up-left, top-right→up, bottom-left→left, bottom-right→no move
- Yellow square click had no effect
- Goal still unclear. Need to figure out target positions.
- Current positions: id=9 [34-35, 15-16], id=10 [35-36, 39-40], id=11 [52-53, 47-48], id=12 [53-54, 12-13]
Plan: Move id=10 toward the blue blob (up and left) to test if touching the blob triggers something.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move id=10 toward the blue blob by clicking its top-left corner (up-left)
# Current: [35-36, 39-40], top-left is [35, 39]
# Move it up-left 5 times to get closer to the blue blob
moves = []
r, c = 35, 39
for i in range(5):
    moves.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c})
    r -= 1
    c -= 1

predict(expect_change=True, note='Move id=10 up-left 5 times toward blue blob')
action(moves)
print("Result:", last_action_result)
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Magenta nodes after moves:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 4:
        print(f"  id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
